project: apra-fleet #204 — Compress skill files using caveman mode

tasks.title: Install caveman as a Claude Code skill on the dev machine
tasks.notes: Installed via: gemini extensions install https://github.com/JuliusBrussee/caveman

tasks.title: VERIFY: Phase 1 — /caveman slash command available in Claude Code
tasks.notes: Caveman available as Gemini extension on fleet-dev2. PM verified install completed successfully.

tasks.title: Compress skills/fleet/*.md (8 files)
tasks.notes: 1870 -> 957 words (48.8% reduction). Escape sequence corruption found and fixed in T6.

tasks.title: Compress skills/pm/ operational files (9 files)
tasks.notes: 6616 -> 1533 words (76.8% reduction). Semantic regressions found and fixed in T6.

tasks.title: Compress skills/pm/tpl-*.md template files (11 files)
tasks.notes: 2157 -> 831 words (61.5% reduction). Escape sequence corruption found and fixed in T6.

tasks.title: VERIFY: Phase 2 — All 28 files compressed ≥40% word-count reduction
tasks.notes: ~68% total reduction across all 28 files. APPROVED.

tasks.title: Risk review pass — write skills/COMPRESSION_REVIEW.md
tasks.notes: 10 high-risk findings identified and resolved. COMPRESSION_REVIEW.md committed.

tasks.title: VERIFY: Phase 3 — Zero unresolved high-risk findings
tasks.notes: All 10 high-risk findings verified fixed. APPROVED.

tasks.title: Regression test — 4 representative PM commands verified correct
tasks.notes: Regressions found: escape sequence corruption (\r, \f, \t, \a in skill files) and semantic loss in PM operational files. Fixed globally. npm run build passes.

tasks.title: VERIFY: Phase 4 — npm run build, npm test, and regression all pass
